description:
You are a monitoring specialist who has just installed a lightweight
alert-daemon on a brand-new Linux host.  
Your next step is to write its first alert policy and produce a small
confirmation log that the deployment system can later parse.

REQUIREMENTS
1. Create the directory “/home/user/monitor/conf”.  
   • Create parent directories if they do not already exist.  
   • No super-user privileges are allowed; everything must remain inside
     /home/user.

2. Inside that directory create the plain-text file
   “/home/user/monitor/conf/alerts.conf” containing exactly four
   newline-separated key=value pairs in the precise order shown below
   (one pair per line, no extra spaces):
        CPU_THRESHOLD=85
        DISK_THRESHOLD=92
        NOTIFY_EMAIL=monitoring@corp.local
        ENABLED=true

3. After writing the file, generate the confirmation log
   “/home/user/monitor/setup.log”.  
   • The log must contain ONE and ONLY one line, terminated with a
     single “\n”.  
   • That single line must read exactly:
        ALERTS_CONFIGURED
   • Nothing else (no timestamps, no spaces, no trailing blank lines).

4. Ensure both newly created files are world-readable
   (chmod 644 is sufficient).

WHAT THE TEST WILL CHECK
• The directory “/home/user/monitor/conf” exists.  
• The file “/home/user/monitor/conf/alerts.conf” exists and its content
  matches *exactly* the four lines listed above, in that order, with a
  trailing newline after the last line.  
• The file “/home/user/monitor/setup.log” exists, is non-empty, contains
  exactly the single line “ALERTS_CONFIGURED\n”, and nothing else.  
• File permissions are 644 (or broader) so that the files are
  world-readable.

No other actions, services, or files are required.  A total of three to
four short shell commands is enough to satisfy all requirements.
truth:
EXPECTED FILES AND CONTENTS

1. Directory:
   Path: /home/user/monitor/conf
   Permissions: 755

2. File:
   Path: /home/user/monitor/conf/alerts.conf
   Permissions: 644
   Exact byte-for-byte content (including final newline):
        CPU_THRESHOLD=85\n
        DISK_THRESHOLD=92\n
        NOTIFY_EMAIL=monitoring@corp.local\n
        ENABLED=true\n

3. File:
   Path: /home/user/monitor/setup.log
   Permissions: 644
   Exact byte-for-byte content (including final newline):
        ALERTS_CONFIGURED\n
